title: Cerasus superflua
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Cerasus superflua". However, I found a plant called "Prunus cerasus", which is also known as the sour cherry.

Here's a paragraph about Prunus cerasus:

Prunus cerasus, commonly known as the sour cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The health benefits of Prunus cerasus include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Prunus cerasus include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Prunus cerasus include teas, juices, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
parts: Cerasus superflua does not seem to be a valid species of plant, however, I found that Cerasus is a genus of flowering plants in the rose family, and Cerasus pseudoforibunda is a species of flowering plant. Cerasus pseudoforibunda's  medicinal parts are not well documented. However, some species of Cerasus have been used in traditional medicine. Cerasus species generally have edible fruits and flowers. The flowers, leaves, and fruits of Cerasus species are sometimes used as herbal remedies.
